channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Republicans call off vote on Iran war resolution that was on the verge of passing
item.description: Republicans struggled Thursday to find the votes to dismiss legislation that would compel President Donald Trump to withdraw from the war with Iran, delaying planned votes on the matter into June.
item.content:encoded:
Republicans struggled Thursday to find the votes to dismiss legislation that would compel President Donald Trump to withdraw from the war with Iran, delaying planned votes on the matter into June.

(Image credit: Mariam Zuhaib)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: After nearly a year at sea, the USS Ford finally comes home
item.description: The crew of the aircraft USS Gerald R. Ford was greeted by thousands of cheering family and friends as part of a process to ease sailors through a difficult transition after being away for months.
item.content:encoded: The crew of the aircraft USS Gerald R. Ford was greeted by thousands of cheering family and friends as part of a process to ease sailors through a difficult transition after being away for months.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: A stranger's compliment helped her feel confident after chemo
item.description: When Pat Gentile began to grow out her hair after chemotherapy, she was nervous to go to work for the first time without a wig. An unexpected encounter with a convenience store stranger changed that.
item.content:encoded: When Pat Gentile began to grow out her hair after chemotherapy, she was nervous to go to work for the first time without a wig. An unexpected encounter with a convenience store stranger changed that.

item.title: Democrats wanted answers for what went wrong in 2024. Now, there are more questions
item.description: The Democratic Party wanted a review of the 2024 election. Then, the DNC learned the report was incomplete and unverifiable, party chairman Ken Martin said Thursday, releasing an annotated version.
item.content:encoded:
The Democratic Party wanted a review of the 2024 election. Then, the DNC learned the report was incomplete and unverifiable, party chairman Ken Martin said Thursday, releasing an annotated version.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Susan Collins brings federal dollars to Maine. She's hoping that's worth it to voters
item.description: As Maine's Senate matchup is all but set, incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins urges voters to pick her over Democrat Graham Platner because she can fund state priorities due to her seniority.
item.content:encoded:
As Maine's Senate matchup is all but set, incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins urges voters to pick her over Democrat Graham Platner because she can fund state priorities due to her seniority.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Steve Mistler

item.title: Colorado Democratic Party censures Gov. Jared Polis over commutation for Tina Peters
item.description: Colorado's Democratic Party voted to formally censure Gov. Jared Polis for commuting the sentence of Tina Peters, a former county clerk convicted over a plot to promote false 2020 election claims.
item.content:encoded: Colorado's Democratic Party voted to formally censure Gov. Jared Polis for commuting the sentence of Tina Peters, a former county clerk convicted over a plot to promote false 2020 election claims.
item.dc:creator: Bente Birkeland

item.title: Amid allegations of abuse on Epstein's 'Zorro Ranch,' New Mexico opens new probes
item.description: Epstein owned a 10,000-acre property with a mansion. After calls by the public, the state attorney general searched the property and the state House created a "Truth Commission."
item.content:encoded:
Epstein owned a 10,000-acre property with a mansion. After calls by the public, the state attorney general searched the property and the state House created a "Truth Commission."

(Image credit: Roberto E. Rosales)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Montaño

item.title: TSA's new 'Gold+' program looks to increase private security screening at airports
item.description: The agency calls the program an update to the Screening Partnership Program, in which 20 U.S. airports currently use private security screeners rather than federal workers.
item.content:encoded:
The agency calls the program an update to the Screening Partnership Program, in which 20 U.S. airports currently use private security screeners rather than federal workers.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: COVID is shaping Americans' reaction to Ebola and hantavirus
item.description: Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.
item.content:encoded:
Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Spencer Pratt is 'winning the internet,' but can he become mayor of Los Angeles?
item.description: Pratt, a former reality TV star, is flooding social media with edgy humor, AI slop and combative rhetoric as a way of grabbing attention and winning the vote of the very online. It's a strategy some political experts see as the future of online campaigning.
item.content:encoded:
Pratt, a former reality TV star, is flooding social media with edgy humor, AI slop and combative rhetoric as a way of grabbing attention and winning the vote of the very online. It's a strategy some political experts see as the future of online campaigning.

(Image credit: Andy Kropa)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn
